questions.text: Which of these is a consequence of air pollution?
questions.explanation: Air pollution causes global warming (greenhouse gases), acid rain (SO₂, NO₂), and respiratory diseases in humans.

questions.text: Water pollution can be prevented by treating industrial waste.
questions.explanation: Treating industrial waste before disposal prevents water pollution by removing harmful chemicals and pollutants.

questions.text: What is the main cause of soil pollution?
questions.correctAnswer: Excessive use of fertilizers, pesticides, and industrial waste disposal.
questions.explanation: Soil pollution is mainly caused by excessive use of chemical fertilizers, pesticides, and improper disposal of industrial and household waste.

questions.text: The process of planting trees is called ________.
questions.explanation: Afforestation is the process of planting trees in areas where there were no forests before, helping restore the environment.
